Name: Rhesus Macaque
Details: The Rhesus macaque is one of the best-known species of Old World monkeys. They are highly adaptable and are used extensively in medical and biological research.

Name: Emperor Tamarin
Details: Named for its resemblance to the German Emperor Wilhelm II, this small monkey is famous for its long, elegant white mustache.

Name: Spider Monkey
Details: Spider monkeys are New World monkeys belonging to the genus Ateles. They have disproportionately long limbs and long prehensile tails, making them highly agile.

Name: Gelada
Details: Sometimes called the bleeding-heart monkey due to the bright red patch on its chest, the gelada is a species of Old World monkey found only in the high grassland of Ethiopia.

Name: Pygmy Marmoset
Details: The pygmy marmoset is a small genus of New World monkey native to rainforests of the western Amazon Basin. It is notable for being the smallest monkey in the world.

Name: Vervet Monkey
Details: The vervet monkey is an Old World monkey with a grey body and a distinct black face fringed with white hair. They are highly social animals.

Name: De Brazza's Monkey
Details: An Old World monkey endemic to the wetlands of central Africa. It is known for its distinct white beard and yellowish-grey crown.

Name: Black-and-white Colobus
Details: Colobus monkeys are distinct for lacking thumbs. They have beautiful contrasting black and white coats and are highly arboreal, rarely descending to the ground.

Name: Night Monkey
Details: Also known as owl monkeys, they are the only truly nocturnal monkeys. They have large brown eyes that help them see in the dark.

Name: Lion-tailed Macaque
Details: An Old World monkey characterized by its outstanding silver-white mane and a tail with a black tuft at the end, similar to a lion's.

Name: Cotton-top Tamarin
Details: A small New World monkey weighing less than 0.5 kg. It is easily recognized by the long white sagittal crest extending from its forehead to its shoulders.

Name: Bald Uakari
Details: A small New World monkey characterized by a very short tail, a bright, crimson face, a bald head, and a long coat.

Name: Patas Monkey
Details: A ground-dwelling monkey known for its incredible speed. It is the fastest primate, capable of running up to 55 km/h.
